intents: create a new user with phone {phone} and name {name}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO users (phone, name, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, NOW())

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT id, phone, name, bio, created_at FROM users WHERE phone = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE users SET bio = $2, updated_at = NOW() WHERE name = $1

authenticate {phone} with code {code} and create session

args.value: INSERT INTO connections (requester_id, recipient_id, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, 'pending', NOW())

args.value: notifications

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE connections SET status = 'accepted', updated_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT id, requester_id, created_at FROM connections WHERE recipient_id = $1 AND status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at DESC

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO messages (sender_id, recipient_id, content, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, 'sent', NOW())

args.name: channel
args.value: chat

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT id, sender_id, content, status, created_at FROM messages WHERE chat_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at ASC

intents: mark messages as read in chat {chat_id}

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE messages SET status = 'read', read_at = NOW() WHERE chat_id = $1 AND status = 'sent'

intents: create appointment between {client} and {provider} for {service} at {time}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO appointments (client_id, provider_id, service, scheduled_at, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, 'pending', NOW())

args.name: channel
args.value: notifications

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE appointments SET status = 'confirmed', updated_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT id, client_id, provider_id, service, scheduled_at, status FROM appointments WHERE client_id = $1 OR provider_id = $1 ORDER BY scheduled_at ASC

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE appointments SET status = 'cancelled', updated_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT u.id, u.name, u.bio, us.price, us.rating_avg FROM users u JOIN user_services us ON u.id = us.user_id WHERE us.service_name = $1 AND u.is_provider = true ORDER BY us.rating_avg DESC

intents: search for providers near {location} within {radius} kilometers

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT u.id, u.name, u.bio, u.lat, u.lon FROM users u WHERE u.is_provider = true AND earth_distance(ll_to_earth(u.lat, u.lon), ll_to_earth($1, $2)) <= $3 * 1000

leave a {rating} star review for appointment {apt_id}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO reviews (appointment_id, rating, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, NOW())

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT r.id, r.rating, r.comment, r.created_at, a.service FROM reviews r JOIN appointments a ON r.appointment_id = a.id WHERE a.provider_id = $1 ORDER BY r.created_at DESC

args.value: user:profile

create a new account with phone number {phone}
sign up a user with phone {phone}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO users (phone, role, created_at) VALUES ($1, 'client', NOW()) ON CONFLICT (phone) DO NOTHING

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT id, phone, name, bio, photo_url, role, is_verified, created_at FROM users WHERE phone = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE users SET bio = $2 WHERE id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT us.service_id, sc.name_en, us.rate_amount, us.rate_currency, us.rate_type FROM user_services us JOIN service_categories sc ON us.service_id = sc.id WHERE us.user_id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE users SET photo_url = $2 WHERE id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE users SET locale = $2 WHERE id = $1

intents: view user {user_id} details with provider info
fetch profile and provider details for {user_id}

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT u.id, u.phone, u.name, u.bio, u.photo_url, u.role, u.is_verified, pp.bio_extended, pp.certifications, pp.working_schedule, pp.gallery FROM users u LEFT JOIN provider_profiles pp ON u.id = pp.user_id WHERE u.id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO connections (requester_id, recipient_id, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, 'pending', NOW())

args.name: channel
args.value: notifications

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE connections SET status = 'accepted' WHERE requester_id = $1 AND recipient_id = $2 AND status = 'pending'

args.name: channel
args.value: notifications

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO blocked_users (blocker_id, blocked_id, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, NOW()) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE connections SET status = 'blocked' WHERE (requester_id = $1 AND recipient_id = $2) OR (requester_id = $2 AND recipient_id = $1)

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT c.id, u.name, u.photo_url, u.bio, c.created_at FROM connections c JOIN users u ON (c.requester_id = u.id OR c.recipient_id = u.id) WHERE (c.requester_id = $1 OR c.recipient_id = $1) AND c.status = 'accepted' AND u.id != $1 ORDER BY u.name ASC

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE connections SET status = 'declined' WHERE id = $1 AND status = 'pending'

intents: add a note for contact {contact_id} saying {note}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO contact_notes (user_id, contact_id, note_text, updated_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW()) ON CONFLICT (user_id, contact_id) DO UPDATE SET note_text = $3, updated_at = NOW()

pool: Great hair stylist, always on time

intents: send a text message in chat {chat_id} saying {content}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO messages (chat_id, sender_id, type, content, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, 'text', $3, 'sent', NOW())

args.name: channel
args.value: chat

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT m.id, m.sender_id, u.name AS sender_name, m.type, m.content, m.media_url, m.status, m.created_at FROM messages m JOIN users u ON m.sender_id = u.id WHERE m.chat_id = $1 AND m.deleted_at IS NULL ORDER BY m.created_at ASC

intents: mark messages as read in chat {chat_id}
set all messages read in chat {chat_id}

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE messages SET status = 'read' WHERE chat_id = $1 AND sender_id != $2 AND status != 'read'

args.name: key
args.value: unread:count

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT COUNT(*) AS unread_count FROM messages m JOIN chat_members cm ON m.chat_id = cm.chat_id WHERE cm.user_id = $1 AND m.sender_id != $1 AND m.status = 'sent'

intents: send an appointment card in chat {chat_id} for {service} at {time}
share appointment proposal in chat {chat_id} for {service} at {time}
post appointment card to chat {chat_id} for {service} at {time}
propose appointment in chat {chat_id} for {service} at {time}
send booking card to chat {chat_id} for {service} at {time}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO appointments (chat_id, creator_id, service, start_time, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, 'proposed', NOW()) RETURNING id

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO messages (chat_id, sender_id, type, content, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, 'appointment_card', $3, NOW())

args.name: channel
args.value: chat

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE messages SET deleted_at = NOW() WHERE id = $1 AND sender_id = $2

intents: create a new chat with user {user_id}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO chats (type, created_by, created_at) VALUES ('direct', $1, NOW()) RETURNING id

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO chat_members (chat_id, user_id, role, joined_at) VALUES ($1, $2, 'member', NOW()), ($1, $3, 'member', NOW())

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT c.id, c.type, c.name, c.photo_url, (SELECT content FROM messages WHERE chat_id = c.id ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1) AS last_message, (SELECT created_at FROM messages WHERE chat_id = c.id ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1) AS last_message_at FROM chats c JOIN chat_members cm ON c.id = cm.chat_id WHERE cm.user_id = $1 ORDER BY last_message_at DESC NULLS LAST

intents: book an appointment with provider {provider_id} on {time} for {service}
make a booking with {provider_id} for {service} on {time}

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT id FROM appointments WHERE provider_id = $1 AND start_time = $2 AND status IN ('proposed', 'confirmed', 'in_progress')

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO appointments (client_id, provider_id, service, start_time, status, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, 'proposed', NOW()) RETURNING id

args.value: notifications

intents: check provider {provider_id} availability for next week
show available slots for {provider_id} next week

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT pp.working_schedule FROM provider_profiles pp WHERE pp.user_id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT start_time, end_time, status FROM appointments WHERE provider_id = $1 AND start_time >= NOW() AND start_time < NOW() + INTERVAL '7 days' AND status IN ('proposed', 'confirmed') ORDER BY start_time ASC

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE appointments SET status = 'cancelled' WHERE id = $1 AND (client_id = $2 OR provider_id = $2)

args.name: channel
args.value: notifications

intents: what appointments do I have coming up

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT a.id, a.service, a.start_time, a.end_time, a.status, a.location, u.name AS provider_name FROM appointments a JOIN users u ON a.provider_id = u.id WHERE a.client_id = $1 AND a.start_time >= NOW() AND a.status IN ('proposed', 'confirmed') ORDER BY a.start_time ASC

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE appointments SET status = 'confirmed' WHERE id = $1 AND status = 'proposed'

args.name: channel
args.value: notifications

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE appointments SET status = 'completed' WHERE id = $1 AND status = 'confirmed'

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE appointments SET start_time = $2, status = 'proposed' WHERE id = $1 AND status IN ('proposed', 'confirmed')

args.name: channel
args.value: notifications

intents: what are the details of appointment {apt_id}

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT a.id, a.service, a.start_time, a.end_time, a.status, a.location, a.rate_amount, a.rate_currency, a.notes, uc.name AS client_name, up.name AS provider_name FROM appointments a JOIN users uc ON a.client_id = uc.id JOIN users up ON a.provider_id = up.id WHERE a.id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT a.id, a.service, a.start_time, a.status, u.name AS client_name, r.rating FROM appointments a JOIN users u ON a.client_id = u.id LEFT JOIN reviews r ON r.appointment_id = a.id WHERE a.provider_id = $1 AND a.status = 'completed' ORDER BY a.start_time DESC

intents: leave a {rating} star review for provider {provider_id} on appointment {apt_id}
rate provider {provider_id} with {rating} stars for appointment {apt_id}
review provider {provider_id} with {rating} stars after appointment {apt_id}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO reviews (appointment_id, reviewer_id, reviewed_id, rating, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, NOW())

intents: leave a review with feedback for appointment {apt_id} rating {rating} comment {feedback}
review appointment {apt_id} with {rating} stars and feedback {feedback}
write review for appointment {apt_id} score {rating} text {feedback}

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO reviews (appointment_id, reviewer_id, rating, feedback, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, NOW())

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT r.id, r.rating, r.feedback, r.tags, r.created_at, u.name AS reviewer_name, a.service FROM reviews r JOIN appointments a ON r.appointment_id = a.id JOIN users u ON r.reviewer_id = u.id WHERE r.reviewed_id = $1 ORDER BY r.created_at DESC

intents: what is the average score of {provider_id}

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT AVG(r.rating)::DECIMAL(3,2) AS avg_rating, COUNT(r.id) AS review_count FROM reviews r WHERE r.reviewed_id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE reviews SET response = $2 WHERE id = $1

intents: who does hair near my location

args.name: address
args.value: current_location

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT u.id, u.name, u.photo_url, u.bio, pp.avg_response_time FROM users u JOIN user_services us ON u.id = us.user_id JOIN service_categories sc ON us.service_id = sc.id LEFT JOIN provider_profiles pp ON u.id = pp.user_id WHERE sc.name_en = 'Hair Styling' AND u.role IN ('provider', 'both') ORDER BY pp.avg_response_time ASC NULLS LAST

intents: find plumbers near me within {radius} kilometers
who does plumbing within {radius}km of here

args.name: address
args.value: current_location

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT u.id, u.name, u.photo_url, u.bio, u.location_lat, u.location_lon, us.rate_amount, us.rate_currency FROM users u JOIN user_services us ON u.id = us.user_id JOIN service_categories sc ON us.service_id = sc.id WHERE sc.name_en = 'Plumbing' AND u.role IN ('provider', 'both') AND earth_distance(ll_to_earth(u.location_lat, u.location_lon), ll_to_earth($1, $2)) <= $3 * 1000

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT u.id, u.name, u.photo_url, u.bio, us.rate_amount, us.rate_currency, us.rate_type, (SELECT AVG(r.rating) FROM reviews r WHERE r.reviewed_id = u.id) AS avg_rating, (SELECT COUNT(r.id) FROM reviews r WHERE r.reviewed_id = u.id) AS review_count FROM users u JOIN user_services us ON u.id = us.user_id JOIN service_categories sc ON us.service_id = sc.id WHERE sc.name_en = $1 AND u.role IN ('provider', 'both') ORDER BY avg_rating DESC NULLS LAST

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT u.id, u.name, u.photo_url, u.bio, u.is_verified FROM users u WHERE u.name ILIKE '%' || $1 || '%' AND u.role IN ('provider', 'both') ORDER BY u.is_verified DESC, u.name ASC

intents: who are the highest rated {service} providers

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT u.id, u.name, u.photo_url, AVG(r.rating)::DECIMAL(3,2) AS avg_rating, COUNT(r.id) AS review_count FROM users u JOIN user_services us ON u.id = us.user_id JOIN service_categories sc ON us.service_id = sc.id LEFT JOIN reviews r ON r.reviewed_id = u.id WHERE sc.name_en = $1 AND u.role IN ('provider', 'both') GROUP BY u.id, u.name, u.photo_url HAVING COUNT(r.id) >= 1 ORDER BY avg_rating DESC LIMIT 10

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT c.id, c.name_en, c.icon, p.name_en AS parent_name FROM service_categories c LEFT JOIN service_categories p ON c.parent_id = p.id ORDER BY p.name_en NULLS FIRST, c.name_en ASC

args.name: sql
args.value: SELECT id, type, title, body, data, read, created_at FROM notifications WHERE user_id = $1 ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 50

args.name: sql
args.value: UPDATE notifications SET read = TRUE WHERE id = $1 AND user_id = $2

args.value: INSERT INTO user_services (user_id, service_id, rate_amount, rate_currency, rate_type) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, 'EUR', 'hourly') ON CONFLICT (user_id, service_id) DO UPDATE SET rate_amount = $3

args.value: UPDATE users SET role = 'provider' WHERE id = $1

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO provider_profiles (user_id) VALUES ($1) ON CONFLICT (user_id) DO NOTHING

args.value: INSERT INTO services_history (appointment_id, hours, rate, total_amount, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $2 * $3, NOW())

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO contact_folders (user_id, name, position) VALUES ($1, $2, (SELECT COALESCE(MAX(position), 0) + 1 FROM contact_folders WHERE user_id = $1))

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO contact_folder_members (folder_id, contact_id) VALUES ($1, $2) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO devices (user_id, device_type, push_token, last_active, created_at) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, NOW(), NOW()) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING

args.name: sql
args.value: INSERT INTO user_settings (user_id, key, value) VALUES ($1, $2, $3) ON CONFLICT (user_id, key) DO UPDATE SET value = $3
